revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:2 nguŏk 4 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:2月4日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 2월 4일]][[zh-min-nan:2 goe̍h 4 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh-yue:二月初四]][[zh:二月初四]]
{{Not to be confused with|February 4|qq=February 4}}<!--why is this category always showing up as 347 members?-->
{{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=二月初四|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 2월 4일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar February|qq=}} '''February 4''' is the 4th day of the month {{ill|February (Lunisolar)|ja|2月 (旧暦)|lt=February (Kisaragi)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/February (Lunisolar)|qq=}}, the 2nd month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}. Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Taian|qid=Q96759844|1=taian}}{{efn|ja|大安|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taian|1=taian|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1306255}}. == Events == * {{ill|Emperor Itoku|en|Emperor Itoku|qid=Q271536|lt=Itotoku Tennō|1=Emperor Itoku}} 1st year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|qid=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar}} {{ill|510 BC|en|510 BC|qid=Q176530|lt=510 BC|1=510 BC}} {{ill|March 15|en|March 15|qid=Q2403|lt=March 15|1=March 15}}) – The 4th Emperor, {{ill|Emperor Itoku|en|Emperor Itoku|qid=Q271536|lt=Itotoku Tennō|1=Emperor Itoku}}, ascended. * {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|景帝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Keitai|1=Emperor Jing|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q841605}} 1st year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|qid=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar}} {{ill|507|en|507|qid=Q32917|lt=507|1=507}} {{ill|March 3|en|March 3|qid=Q2391|lt=March 3|1=March 3}}) – The crown prince, known as Otoko Taikyaku Ō, ascended as the 26th Emperor, {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|景帝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Keitai|1=Emperor Jing|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q841605}}. * {{ill|Jinki (era)|en|Jinki (era)|qid=Q1373210|lt=Jinki|1=Jinki (era)}} 1st year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|qid=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar}} {{ill|724|en|724|qid=Q23549|lt=724|1=724}} {{ill|March 3|en|March 3|qid=Q2391|lt=March 3|1=March 3}}) – Owing to the auspicious appearance of the white tortoise, the era name was changed from {{ill|Yōrō|en|Yōrō|qid=Q1373061|lt=Yōrō|1=Yōrō}} to {{ill|Jinki (era)|en|Jinki (era)|qid=Q1373210|lt=Jinki|1=Jinki (era)}}. * In the same {{ill|Jinki (era)|en|Jinki (era)|qid=Q1373210|lt=Jinki|1=Jinki (era)}} 1st year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|qid=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar}} {{ill|724|en|724|qid=Q23549|lt=724|1=724}} {{ill|March 3|en|March 3|qid=Q2391|lt=March 3|1=March 3}}) – {{ill|Empress Genshō|en|Empress Genshō|qid=Q235343|lt=Genchō Tennō|1=Empress Genshō}} abdicated, and his first-born became Emperor Shōmu, the 45th Emperor. * {{ill|Tenpyō-shōhō|en|Tenpyō-shōhō|qid=Q1373169|lt=Tenpyō Shōhō|1=Tenpyō-shōhō}} 6th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|qid=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar}} {{ill|754|en|754|qid=Q23646|lt=754|1=754}} {{ill|March 2|en|March 2|qid=Q2390|lt=March 2|1=March 2}}) – A high monk from the Tang Dynasty, {{ill|Jianzhen|en|Jianzhen|qid=Q714061|lt=Ganjin|1=Jianzhen}}, arrived in the capital of Nara. * {{ill|Bun'ei|en|Bun'ei|qid=Q1010103|lt=Bunmei|1=Bun'ei}} 14th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|qid=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1482|en|1482|qid=Q6633|lt=1482|1=1482}} {{ill|February 21|en|February 21|qid=Q2350|lt=February 21|1=February 21}}) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimasa|en|Ashikaga Yoshimasa|qid=Q447034|lt=Ashikaga Yoshimasa|1=Ashikaga Yoshimasa}} began the construction of Jishō-ji Kannon-dō (the Silver Pavilion, 慈照寺観音殿) in Higashiyama. * {{ill|Keichō|en|Keichō|qid=Q1328185|lt=Keichō|1=Keichō}} 9th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|qid=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1604|en|1604|qid=Q6738|lt=1604|1=1604}} {{ill|March 4|en|March 4|qid=Q2396|lt=March 4|1=March 4}}) – The Tokugawa shogunate established a system of post stations ({{ill|Ichirizuka|en|Ichirizuka|qid=Q846760|lt=Ichirizuka|1=Ichirizuka}}) along the Tōkaidō, Higashiyama Road, and Hokuriku-dō, using Nihonbashi in Edo as the starting point. * {{ill|Genroku|en|Genroku|qid=Q1325001|lt=Genroku|1=Genroku}} 16th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|qid=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1703|en|1703|qid=Q6822|lt=1703|1=1703}} {{ill|March 20|en|March 20|qid=Q2427|lt=March 20|1=March 20}}) – The Tokugawa shogunate ordered 46 of the Akō rōshi to commit seppuku. * {{ill|Bunkyū|en|Bunkyū|qid=Q730266|lt=Bunkyū|1=Bunkyū}} 1st year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|qid=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1861|en|1861|qid=Q7693|lt=1861|1=1861}} {{ill|March 14|en|March 14|qid=Q2404|lt=March 14|1=March 14}}) – The Russian warship 【Posadnik】 arrived at Tsushima, and soldiers landed at Imozaki to occupy it; they withdrew in August. == Births == * {{ill|Kaei|en|Kaei|qid=Q1146125|lt=Kaei|1=Kaei}} 6th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|qid=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1853|en|1853|qid=Q7666|lt=1853|1=1853}} {{ill|March 13|en|March 13|qid=Q2400|lt=March 13|1=March 13}}) – {{ill|Kaneko Kentarō|en|Kaneko Kentarō|qid=Q3192621|lt=Kaneko Kentarō|1=Kaneko Kentarō}}, a bureaucrat and politician (died {{ill|1942|en|1942|qid=Q18625|lt=1942|1=1942}}). * {{ill|Ansei|en|Ansei|qid=Q1327259|lt=Ansei|1=Ansei}} 5th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|qid=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1858|en|1858|qid=Q7684|lt=1858|1=1858}} {{ill|March 18|en|March 18|qid=Q2422|lt=March 18|1=March 18}}) – {{ill|Inoue Enryō|en|Inoue Enryō|qid=Q1414939|lt=Inoue Enryo|1=Inoue Enryō}}, a Buddhist philosopher who founded a predecessor of Tokyo University of Foreign Studies (died {{ill|1919|en|1919|qid=Q2157|lt=1919|1=1919}}). * {{ill|Genji era|en|Genji era|qid=Q1325419|lt=Genji|1=Genji era}} 2nd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|qid=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1865|en|1865|qid=Q7708|lt=1865|1=1865}} {{ill|March 1|en|March 1|qid=Q2393|lt=March 1|1=March 1}}) – {{ill|Abe Isoo|en|Abe Isoo|qid=Q318151|lt=Abe Isoo|1=Abe Isoo}}, a critic and socialist activist (died {{ill|1949|en|1949|qid=Q5188|lt=1949|1=1949}}). * In Genji 2nd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|qid=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} 1865, {{ill|March 1|en|March 1|qid=Q2393|lt=March 1|1=March 1}}) – {{ill|Shiratori Kurakichi|en|Shiratori Kurakichi|qid=Q1188365|lt=Hakuchō Koyoshi|1=Shiratori Kurakichi}}, an oriental historian (died {{ill|1942|en|1942|qid=Q18625|lt=1942|1=1942}}). == Deaths == * {{ill|Yōwa|en|Yōwa|qid=Q729609|lt=Yōwa|1=Yōwa}} 1st year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|qid=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1181|en|1181|qid=Q19704|lt=1181|1=1181}} {{ill|February 19|en|February 19|qid=Q2340|lt=February 19|1=February 19}}) – {{ill|Taira no Kiyomori|en|Taira no Kiyomori|qid=Q281833|lt=Taira no Kiyomori|1=Taira no Kiyomori}}, a military commander ({{ill|Busho (Rank)|ja|武将|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Busho (Rank)|1=military commander|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11545923}}) and Chancellor ({{ill|Minister (government)|en|Minister (government)|qid=Q83307|lt=Daijō-daijin|1=Minister (government)}}) (born {{ill|1118|en|1118|qid=Q19539|lt=1118|1=1118}}). * In Genroku 16th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|qid=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1703|en|1703|qid=Q6822|lt=1703|1=1703}} {{ill|March 20|en|March 20|qid=Q2427|lt=March 20|1=March 20}}) – {{ill|Ōishi Yoshio|en|Ōishi Yoshio|qid=Q530128|lt=Ōishi Yoshio|1=Ōishi Yoshio}} ordered the seppuku of the 46 Akō rōshi. * {{ill|Tōchi|ja|同治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōchi|1=Tongzhi Era|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1135837}} 11th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|qid=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1872|en|1872|qid=Q7753|lt=1872|1=1872}} {{ill|March 12|en|March 12|qid=Q2402|lt=March 12|1=March 12}}) – An official of the former Tokugawa shogunate in the Qing dynasty (born {{ill|1811|en|1811|qid=Q6928|lt=1811|1=1811}}). == Commemorations ==
== See Also == {{Lunar Calendar See Also|2|20|2|22|1|21|3|2|0221|qq=}}
==Notes== <references group="lower-alpha"/> ==References== {{reflist|qq=}} {{Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}
[[zh-min-nan:2 goe̍h 4 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:二月初四]]
[[zh-min-nan:2 goe̍h 21 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:二月廿一]]
{{translated page|ja|2月4日 (旧暦)|version=101715699|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[cdo:2 nguŏk 4 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[cdo:2 nguŏk 21 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[ko:%EC%9D%8C%EB%A0%A5 2%EC%9B%94 4%EC%9D%BC]] [[zh:%E4%BA%8C%E6%9C%88%E5%88%9D%E5%9B%9B]] [[ja:2月4日 (旧暦)]] [[de:February 4 (Lunisolar)]]
[[ko:음력 2월 21일]] [[ja:2月21日 (旧暦)]] [[zh:二月廿一]]
[[ar:February 4 (Lunisolar)]]
